titleStmt.title: The Ambassador in Iran ( (Morris) ) to the Secretary of State

div.head: The Ambassador in Iran ( Morris ) to the Secretary of State
div.p:
18. MedjlissThe Iranian Parliament. today passed billText transmitted to the Department in despatch 177, January 12, 1945, from Tehran, not printed. canceling as of this date the special economic powers granted MillspaughDr. Arthur O. Millspaugh, American Administrator General of the Finances in the Iranian Government. by the law of 13 Ordibehesht.For substance of the law of 13 Ordibehesht, 1322 (May 4, 1943), see telegram 386, April 14, 1943, 5 p.m., from Tehran, Foreign Relations, 1943, vol. iv, p. 522. The text was transmitted to the Department in despatch 556, May 20, 1943, from Tehran (not printed), and has been published by Dr. Millspaugh in his Americans in Persia (Brookings Institution, 1946), p. 273.
Council of Ministers is authorized to dissolve or reallocate the economic organizations which were under Millspaugh administration by virtue of that law and to annul change or retain all regulations issued thereunder.
Cabinet is directed to create high consultative committee for economic affairs to prepare within 3 months an economic plan in conformity with the needs of the country.

Repeated to Cairo for AEMMEAmerican Economic Mission in the Middle East. and Hoskins.Lt. Col. Harold B. Hoskins, Adviser on Economic Affairs, with the rank of Counselor, at Legation in Egypt; assigned concurrently to Missions in Ethiopia, Iran, Iraq, Lebanon, and Saudi Arabia.
